violations.text: The following were in poor repair: 1. Slicer grip was in poor repair; missing pieces were observed. 2. Chest freezer door gaskets were observed torn and in poor repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2026

Various foods were observed held at 44-49 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed of. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, sausage was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC moved to sheet pans at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

The following were in poor repair: 1. Grab & go cooler located in the cafeteria area was not in good repair, ambient observed at 44F. PIC took unit out of service at time of inspection. 2. Slicer grip was in poor repair; missing pieces were observed. 3. Chest freezer door gaskets were observed torn and in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2024

The floor in the dry storage areas have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2024
